brand.name: CHI, The Spa at Shangri-La Bengaluru

campaigns.name: Booking Confirmation, Reminders & No-Show Recovery
campaigns.objective: Maximise attended appointments, prime the sensory experience before arrival, and warmly recover the bookings that slip — protecting revenue against the strict 100% no-show charge.
campaigns.trigger: Booking confirmed via SpaTime; 24h-before and morning-of timers; no-show event; treatment completed.
campaigns.segment: Anyone with a scheduled CHI treatment — in-stay guests and local residents alike.
campaigns.rationale: This is the single highest-urgency gap. A 100% no-show charge with a 4-hour cancellation window creates real resentment when a time-poor guest simply forgets; reminders cut no-shows ~35% on average and let CHI communicate the policy warmly instead of as a threat. The pre-arrival emails also begin the 'aha' — arriving early, the 3rd-floor zen environment, the aromatic oils — before a single hand is laid, which is exactly where this brand's experience starts.

emails.purpose: Confirm the appointment, set sensory expectations, ask the guest to arrive 30 minutes early for the consultation and hydrothermal facilities, and state the cancellation window gently.
emails.subject: Your sanctuary on the 3rd floor is reserved
emails.preview: A few quiet notes to help you arrive ready to let go.
emails.cta: Add your treatment to your calendar
keyPoints: Confirm treatment name, date, time, duration and therapist where known
Invite the guest to arrive 30 minutes early for the health consultation, steam, sauna and Jacuzzi
Suggest leaving phone and schedule behind; the experience begins on arrival
State the 4-hour cancellation window warmly, framed as 'so we can offer your time to another guest'

emails.purpose: Remind the guest the day before, offer a frictionless reschedule path, and deepen anticipation with prep guidance.
emails.subject: Tomorrow, the city stays at the door
emails.preview: What to expect from your CHI ritual — and how to make the most of it.
keyPoints: Restate date and time and the recommended early arrival
Light prep: hydrate, eat lightly beforehand, wear something easy to change from
One-tap reschedule link so a clash never becomes a no-show
A line on the aromatic oils and unhurried pace awaiting them

emails.purpose: Final gentle reminder with directions, the 3rd-floor location and arrival logistics for both hotel guests and local visitors.
emails.subject: Today at {time} — your stillness awaits
emails.preview: Directions to CHI and a note for arriving guests.
keyPoints: Time, treatment and the 3rd-floor location of CHI at Shangri-La Bengaluru
Parking and entry guidance for local non-staying guests (arrive before 10pm)
Reminder to arrive early so nothing about the day feels rushed
Spa contact numbers should anything change last minute

emails.purpose: Recover a missed appointment with warmth and zero penalty in tone, inviting an easy rebooking.
emails.subject: We held your space — shall we find a new time?
emails.preview: No rush. Your reset is still waiting whenever you are.
keyPoints: Acknowledge the day got away from them without judgement
Reassure that rebooking is simple and they remain welcome
One-click link to pick a fresh slot
Offer to call the spa directly if they would prefer a human

emails.purpose: Close the loop after the visit, extend the feeling of restoration, and open the door to the next booking without pressure.
emails.subject: We hope you left a little lighter
emails.preview: A few ways to carry the calm home with you.
keyPoints: Thank them and gently encourage hydration and rest to extend the benefit
Suggest pre-booking the next treatment while the feeling is fresh
Mention the 7-for-5 bundle for those who want to make it a ritual
Invite a reply with any feedback — a real person reads it

campaigns.name: Welcome & Onboarding for New Subscribers and First-Time Guests
campaigns.objective: Turn a new subscriber or curious first-timer into a booked first appointment — the activation event — by building trust in CHI's authenticity and easing the price hesitation.
campaigns.trigger: Newsletter signup, spa-menu download, gift-voucher interest, or Shangri-La Circle enrolment.
campaigns.segment: New contacts who have not yet completed a treatment; branch by hotel guest vs local resident where known.
campaigns.rationale: There is no pre-booking nurture today, yet the large local non-guest audience researches deliberately and hesitates on a considered INR 7K+ spend. A welcome sequence is the fastest way to show CHI's authentic Asian-healing heritage, answer the 'is a hotel spa really as good as a specialist?' objection, and convert intent into a first booking — the moment that activates everything else.

emails.purpose: Warm welcome that sets the unhurried tone and frames CHI as an invitation to arrive, with a single easy first step.
emails.subject: Welcome — consider this an invitation to slow down
emails.preview: The CHI philosophy, and how to find your way to stillness.
keyPoints: Introduce CHI's roots in the universal life force and ancient Asian healing wisdom
Set expectations: short, occasional notes, never noise
First action — browse the menu to sense which ritual calls to them
Reassure a real person is always a reply away

emails.purpose: Lead with the core 'aha' — what a CHI Signature treatment actually feels like, from arrival ritual to emergence.
emails.subject: What it feels like to walk out a different person
emails.preview: Inside a CHI Signature ritual — from the first breath to the last.
keyPoints: Walk through the sensory arc: consultation, aromatic oils, the Swedish-Shiatsu-Reflexology blend
Emphasise lasting restoration, not a quick rub-down
Paint the moment of emergence — grounded, unhurried, restored
Soft CTA to read more or book when ready

emails.purpose: Handle the trust and price objection with heritage and proof — why CHI is worth it versus a standalone spa.
emails.subject: Why a single treatment is worth the considered choice
emails.preview: On authenticity, and what the price truly holds.
keyPoints: Address the 'hotel spa vs specialist' doubt with CHI's global standard and trained therapists
Frame the price as the full ritual — facilities, consultation, time that is truly yours
Echo how guests describe it: 'worth every rupee', 'we didn't want to leave'
Reassure without urgency

emails.purpose: Educate so the guest can choose with confidence — demystify the Ayurvedic therapies and help match a treatment to their need.
emails.subject: Abhyanga, Shirodhara, Shiatsu — which calm is yours?
emails.preview: A gentle guide to choosing your first CHI ritual.
emails.cta: Find the ritual that suits you
keyPoints: Plain-language explanation of the signature and Ayurvedic therapies
Help match intent — tension, sleeplessness, fatigue — to a treatment, without referencing health data
Note the option to arrive early and use the hydrothermal facilities
Invite a reply for a recommendation from the team

emails.purpose: Soft nudge toward the first booking with a real offer of help and a low-friction path.
emails.subject: Whenever you're ready, we'll be here
emails.preview: A quiet nudge, and an easy way to begin.
keyPoints: No pressure — acknowledge the right time is theirs to choose
Mention the gift voucher as a way to gift the experience or ease in
Direct, simple booking link and the spa phone numbers
Reiterate that a reply reaches a real person who can advise

campaigns.name: Re-Booking, Occasion & Expansion
campaigns.objective: Drive repeat bookings, raise inter-visit frequency, and grow the value of each guest through longer sessions, bundles, couples' rooms and occasion gifting.
campaigns.trigger: Last visit + ~60 days; birthday/anniversary on file; post-visit upsell readiness; ~90 days dormant.
campaigns.segment: Past guests who have completed at least one treatment; branch by recency and by occasion data where Shangri-La Circle holds it.
campaigns.rationale: With no subscription and no observed re-booking trigger, the happiest existing guests quietly lapse. This is the highest-ROI gap for an established base: occasion emails (birthday, anniversary) and a gentle ~60-day re-booking nudge turn one-off visits into a ritual, while the 90-min upgrade, the 7-for-5 bundle and couples' rooms lift spend per guest.

emails.purpose: Re-booking nudge that recalls how they felt and invites them to renew the reset.
emails.subject: It may be time to feel that way again
emails.preview: Two months on — your next stillness is a moment away.
keyPoints: Recall the feeling of their last visit without referencing health details
Position regular care as a ritual, not an indulgence
Easy booking link with a nod to early arrival for the facilities
Gentle, unhurried tone throughout

emails.purpose: Occasion email inviting them to mark the moment with a treatment — for themselves or as a couple.
emails.subject: A quiet way to mark the occasion
emails.preview: Some moments deserve more than a candle and a cake.
keyPoints: Warm acknowledgement of the upcoming birthday or anniversary
Offer the couples' room for shared celebrations
Suggest the Shankara Sandalwood or seasonal package as a special-occasion choice
No discount language — frame as a fitting way to celebrate

emails.purpose: Introduce ways to deepen the experience — longer sessions, add-ons and the bundle — to high-affinity returning guests.
emails.subject: More time on the table, less on the clock
emails.preview: Ninety minutes, add-on rituals, and a bundle worth keeping.
keyPoints: Invite upgrading 60 to 90 minutes for a deeper reset
Suggest add-ons such as a body scrub before a massage, or a Shirodhara
Present the 7-treatments-for-5 bundle as a way to commit to the ritual at better value
Keep it as an invitation, never a hard sell

emails.purpose: A gentle save-style note before the guest fully lapses, with an easy door back in.
emails.subject: The 3rd floor has been quiet without you
emails.preview: Whenever the city gets too loud, we're still here.
keyPoints: Soft acknowledgement that it has been a while
Re-affirm the value of returning for a genuine reset
Offer to help find a time or answer questions by reply or phone
Mention what's new or seasonal to give a fresh reason to return

campaigns.name: Win-Back & Re-Engagement
campaigns.objective: Reactivate lapsed local guests and dormant subscribers, and respectfully sunset those who don't respond to protect deliverability.
campaigns.trigger: No bookings, opens or clicks for 90+ days.
campaigns.segment: Dormant subscribers and lapsed local non-guests who have no Shangri-La Circle loyalty loop; separate message from never-booked subscribers.
campaigns.rationale: Local non-hotel visitors have no loyalty mechanism, so once they drift they are simply gone. A short win-back recovers 5–15% of inactive contacts and cleans the list. For a serene luxury brand the tone matters most — these emails re-extend the invitation with fresh value, never a desperate plea or a discount blast.

emails.purpose: A warm 'we miss you' that reminds them of the core feeling CHI offers.
emails.subject: Has the city kept you too long?
emails.preview: Your space for stillness hasn't gone anywhere.
keyPoints: Gently note it's been a while since their last visit
Re-evoke the restorative feeling without health references
Low-friction link back to booking
Keep it calm and unhurried, not urgent

emails.purpose: Pattern-interrupt with what's new — fresh treatments, seasonal rituals or collaborations since they left.
emails.subject: What's new on the 3rd floor since you last visited
emails.preview: New rituals and a seasonal collaboration worth knowing about.
keyPoints: Introduce new or seasonal treatments and the Shankara Sandalwood collaboration
Frame as a fresh reason to come back, not a guilt trip
Single clear CTA to explore
Maintain the sensory, aspirational voice

emails.purpose: Offer a thoughtful reason to return — a curated recommendation or gift-voucher value framing.
emails.subject: A little something to welcome you back
emails.preview: A ritual we think you'll love, chosen with care.
keyPoints: Suggest a specific treatment matched to a common need like tension or sleep
Mention the gift voucher as a way to treat themselves or someone dear
Reassure that the experience remains everything they remember
Keep value framing elegant, never bargain-led

emails.purpose: Respectful sunset — confirm whether they'd like to keep hearing from CHI.
emails.subject: Shall we keep in touch?
emails.preview: Only if our notes still bring you something worth keeping.
keyPoints: Politely ask if they'd like to continue receiving CHI's notes
Make staying a single click; make leaving graceful
Thank them sincerely regardless of choice
Suppress non-responders after this send to protect sender reputation

campaigns.name: The CHI Journal — Value Newsletter
campaigns.objective: Stay top of mind between visits and build authority in authentic Asian wellness, with one useful idea per send and a light, occasional invitation to book.
campaigns.segment: All engaged contacts; suppress those in active onboarding and route the recently dormant into Win-Back instead.
campaigns.rationale: There is no regular content email today, leaving long gaps between occasional visits where CHI fades from mind. A monthly journal — seasonal wellness, therapist craft, treatment education — keeps the brand present and reinforces the authenticity that justifies the price, all in an 80% value / 20% soft-offer balance that suits a brand that must never feel salesy.

emails.purpose: Seasonal wellness edition tying Bengaluru's season to a relevant ritual.
emails.subject: Monsoon in the city, stillness on the 3rd floor
emails.preview: A seasonal note on warmth, breath and letting the rains slow you down.
keyPoints: One useful seasonal wellness idea suited to the Bengaluru monsoon
Tie it to a fitting CHI treatment such as a warming Pinda Sweda
Recurring 'This season at CHI' habit section
Single soft CTA to read or book

emails.purpose: Therapist craft spotlight — humanise the expertise behind the treatments.
emails.subject: The hands behind the calm
emails.preview: Meet a CHI therapist on the art of an unhurried touch.
keyPoints: Profile a therapist's training and philosophy of touch
Reinforce authenticity and the global standard versus a generic spa
Behind-the-scenes of the ritual and the environment
Gentle invitation to experience it firsthand

emails.purpose: Treatment education — demystify an Ayurvedic therapy in depth.
emails.subject: Shirodhara: the quiet stream that resets the mind
emails.preview: What this ancient ritual does, and who it's for.
keyPoints: Explain the origins and experience of Shirodhara in evocative, plain language
Describe the kind of stillness it offers, without medical claims
Connect to CHI's broader Ayurvedic menu
Soft CTA to explore or book the treatment

emails.purpose: Occasion and gifting edition around a festive or celebratory season.
emails.subject: The most considered gift you can give this season
emails.preview: On gifting stillness — to someone you love, or to yourself.
keyPoints: Frame the gift of a CHI experience around the festive season
Present gift vouchers and seasonal packages like the Festive Spa Getaway
Note couples' rooms and bundles for shared or generous gifting
Keep it warm and tasteful, with one clear CTA
